carbocation_stability.description: Stability order of carbocations (most → least stable)
jee_traps: Cyclopropylcarbinyl cation is MORE stable than secondary — bisected σ-delocalization
Neopentyl cation (1°) rearranges immediately to tertiary — not observed as primary
Vinyl and phenyl cations are LESS stable than primary alkyl
EWG on benzylic C destabilizes, EDG stabilizes
1-Phenylethyl (benzylic 2°) > 2-phenylethyl (primary with homoconjugation)
exceptions: 2-Norbornyl cation — non-classical; particularly stable due to bridged σ-delocalization

radical_stability.description: Stability order of carbon radicals (most → least stable)
jee_traps: Allylic and benzylic radicals: same tier as corresponding tertiary for JEE purposes
Vinyl radicals are LESS stable than primary alkyl — sp hybridized C, no hyperconjugation
Phenyl radical ≈ vinyl radical in instability

carbanion_stability.description: Stability order of carbanions (most → least stable)
jee_traps: Order is INVERSE of carbocation stability — EWG stabilizes carbanion, EDG destabilizes
sp hybridized C is most acidic → alkynide is most stable carbanion
Tertiary carbanion is LEAST stable (opposite of carbocation!)

acidity.description: Brønsted acidity order (pKa, lower = more acidic)
jee_traps: EWG on carboxylic acid INCREASES acidity (lowers pKa) — CCl3COOH >> CH3COOH
EWG on phenol INCREASES acidity; EDG DECREASES it — para effect stronger than meta
ortho-effect: bulky groups near OH decrease acidity (steric inhibition of solvation)
Inductive vs resonance: inductive fades with distance, resonance is direct
Terminal alkyne pKa ~25: more acidic than alkene (44) or alkane (50) due to sp hybridization
Phenol (pKa ~10) MORE acidic than alcohol (~16) but LESS than carboxylic acid (~5)
exceptions: 2-Nitrophenol is MORE acidic than 4-nitrophenol due to intramolecular H-bonding (reduces solvation of 2-NP anion... actually 4-NP is more acidic — JEE trap: ortho-intramolecular H-bond in 2-NP anion actually stabilizes it less than para)
Picric acid (2,4,6-trinitrophenol) pKa ~0.4 — stronger than benzoic acid

basicity.description: Brønsted basicity order (pKb, lower = more basic; or pKa of conjugate acid, higher = more basic)
jee_traps: In water: secondary amine (R2NH) < tertiary (R3N) — hydration of conjugate acid matters
In gas phase: basicity order is 3° > 2° > 1° > NH3 (pure inductive)
Aniline MUCH weaker base than aliphatic amine — lone pair delocalized into ring
EWG on ring DECREASES aniline basicity; EDG INCREASES it
Amide nitrogen NOT basic — lone pair fully conjugated with C=O
Guanidine is the strongest neutral organic base (pKa of conjugate acid ~13.6)
exceptions: 2,6-di-tert-butylpyridine: weaker base than pyridine despite EDG — steric blockade of N

nucleophilicity_protic.description: Nucleophilicity order in protic solvents (e.g., water, methanol). Order differs from basicity due to solvation.
jee_traps: CRITICAL: In protic solvents, order is I- > Br- > Cl- > F- (inverse of basicity/electronegativity!)
F- is WORST nucleophile in protic solvents — most solvated (smallest ion, strongest H-bond)
I- is BEST — largest, least solvated, most polarizable
Thiolates (RS-) are better nucleophiles than alkoxides (RO-) despite weaker basicity — polarizability wins
CN- is excellent nucleophile (less solvated than OH-) but moderate base

nucleophilicity_aprotic.description: Nucleophilicity order in aprotic solvents (DMSO, DMF, acetone). Parallels basicity — no solvation effect.
jee_traps: CRITICAL: In aprotic solvents, F- > Cl- > Br- > I- (same as basicity order — solvation removed)
This is the INVERTED order relative to protic solvents — a classic JEE trap
RO- (alkoxide) is excellent nucleophile in aprotic — not solvated
Polar aprotic solvents (DMSO, DMF) favor SN2 with hard nucleophiles

leaving_group_ability.description: Leaving group ability in SN reactions (best → worst). Inversely correlated with basicity of the leaving group anion.
jee_traps: OH- is a BAD leaving group — must be protonated to H2O first, or converted to tosylate
F- is a WORSE leaving group than Cl- (opposite of its nucleophilicity in protic solvents)
Triflate (TfO-) is the best LG of all — pKa of TfOH ≈ -14
N2 from diazonium is thermodynamic driving force — irreversible, hence diazonium SN very fast
NR2- cannot leave — too basic, would require impossible carbanion/hydride-like process

sn1_vs_sn2.description: Factors governing SN1 vs SN2 selectivity

substrate_structure.favors_sn1: tertiary > secondary > primary (never methyl — methyl cation too unstable)
substrate_structure.jee_trap: 2° substrates can go either way — solvent and nucleophile concentration determine it

nucleophile.favors_sn1: weak nucleophile or solvent (H2O, ROH) — SN1 is first-order, nucleophile not in rate

solvent.favors_sn2: polar aprotic (DMSO, DMF, acetone) — naked nucleophile
solvent.favors_sn1: polar protic (H2O, MeOH, EtOH) — stabilizes carbocation and anion

stereochemistry.sn1: racemisation (carbocation is planar sp2) with slight excess inversion (~55:45) due to ion pair shielding

jee_traps: SN1 gives racemisation, NOT 100% inversion
SN2 with retention: ONLY via neighbouring group participation (NGP/anchimeric assistance)
Neopentyl bromide: primary but reacts very slowly by SN2 (steric) AND cannot do SN1 (1° cation); rearranges
Allylic and benzylic substrates: SN1 at these positions even for primary/secondary due to resonance stabilisation of cation
exceptions: Neighbouring group participation (NGP): gives retention of configuration via double inversion

alkene_stability.stereo_note: Trans (E) > cis (Z) in stability — less steric strain
jee_traps: More substituted = more stable (hyperconjugation + steric stabilization by induction)
Hofmann product (less substituted) is kinetic product under some conditions — bulky base favors it
Cyclic alkenes: cis-fused cyclohexene more stable than trans (trans-cyclohexene is strained)

eas_reactivity.description: EAS (electrophilic aromatic substitution) reactivity of benzene derivatives
jee_traps: Halogens are DEACTIVATING but ortho/para DIRECTORS — lone pair donation overrides induction for regiochemistry
Strongly deactivated rings may not react under normal EAS conditions at all
Nitro group is meta director AND deactivating; amine is ortho/para director AND activating
Acetanilide: -NHCOR is weaker activator than -NH2 because lone pair partially tied up by C=O
exceptions: Diazonium coupling (Ar-N2+) only with strongly activated rings (phenol, aniline) — very weak electrophile

bond_dissociation_energy.description: C-H BDE order for radical halogenation selectivity

tiers.label: Weakest C-H bond (lowest BDE → fastest radical abstraction)

jee_traps: Radical selectivity: Br• highly selective (thermodynamic, high activation energy) — prefers 3° >> 2° >> 1°
Cl• less selective (kinetic, low activation energy) — gives statistical mixture at fast reactions
Allylic and benzylic: same BDE tier as tertiary — resonance stabilizes radical
Fluorination: not useful (too exothermic, unselective, often explosive)

ring_strain.description: Ring strain in cycloalkanes (most strained → least strained)
jee_traps: Cyclopropane undergoes ring-opening with HX (electrophilic) unlike cyclohexane
Cyclobutane is strained but does NOT open as easily as cyclopropane
Cyclohexane chair has ZERO ring strain — all C-C-C angles at tetrahedral 109.5°
Angle strain + eclipsing strain + torsional strain all contribute

imine_formation_rate.description: Rate of imine (Schiff base) formation from carbonyl + amine

tiers.label: Slowest
members: ketone + secondary amine → enamine (no NH — iminium pathway)

jee_traps: pH optimum ~4–5: too acidic → amine protonated (no nucleophile); too basic → no acid catalyst for dehydration
Secondary amines give ENAMINES, not imines — no N-H to lose in dehydration
